item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as both image's original sources cannot be found and no source in which these images are present indicate they have been released under Creative Commons. Thus, both images have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Death by non-euclidian space-distorting goat entrails. What a way to go.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what a weird death "Man killed by exploding goat guts"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor Dr. Jept, losing his left arm and left leg like that. At least now he's all right… That pun was bad and I don't feel bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't believe the Goat is fucking dead.

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I guess he really liked glue sticks.

I really like this one for showing that even relatively benign or "run of the mill" anomalies can kill the shit out of you for seemingly silly reasons.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … where did it even FIND a giraffe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1 beehive (whole, dormant)

WHAT IF GOATS' NON-EUCLIDEAN STOMACHS WERE BEES

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm confused. When the doctor recommended euthanizing the goat in a controlled environment, I thought, "Oh, so he realized that the anomaly containing the goat's huge amount of mass might cease to be contained once it dies, so he wants to have it killed where it won't harm people with intestines or gravity waves."

Of course, the goat's death kills the researchers (only researchers?) that were about 73 yards away. I'd understand if a few people were injured, seeing that the detonation size was pretty huge— but I expected they'd be more cautious when they were dealing with something more than 10,000 times greater than the mass of an average car.

Also yes I read this cus I saw it matched up with 1860

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Basically, take the inside of the goat. Then, extend it outward to a sphere 67m across. That's the new space that was created. But there's then the problem of the 67m diameter sphere of space that was already there before expansion. That space got distorted and displaced into an even larger area, leading to the effects on Dr. Jept's skeleton and the like.

But otherwise, yes, the distortion was very much like a crushing from an inflation event.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Does "67 meters in diameter" refer to the size of the distorted space before or after the distortion? My initial mental picture of the event had the volume of the goat's insides simply appearing within the goat's outsides, inflating the corpse enormously and impacting its surroundings with extreme force. That appears to match what happened to Dr. Jept and the surrounding area (the impact crater), but it doesn't seem to fit with the description of what happened to the other researchers. They'd effectively have been hit by a curved wall of goat parts traveling… however you measure covering 33.5 meters "instantaneously"; the only "massive anatomical deformation" that would cause is more easily described as blunt-force trauma across their entire bodies.

I do love the idea of guys being killed by new space appearing inside them and deforming their bodies, but the description indicates to me that all the new space appeared inside the goat, and the image of Dr. Jept fits that interpretation. He hasn't been inflated- he's been partially crushed by the inflation of something that pressed against him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent parazoology article. 50/10 because of massive spatial distortion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: http://i.imgur.com/y51lG4y.png

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2282 consumes approximately three cubic meters (3 m³) per day.

There's my upvote. I like how "three cubic meters of what?" is a wrong question to ask here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this demonstrates the whole "this is why the Foundation doesn't just blow everything up" thing very well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a higher bar for Wacky Stuff Found In Thing, so I felt it didn't pay off enough. That said, there was enough there entertaining enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how much awesome screwed-upness you managed to wring out of an old cliche here. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent payoff. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1 giraffe skeleton (whole)

+1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was funny and unsettling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was on the fence on this one. It's a little silly for my tastes, but it's well-written and a decent realization of the concept. Then I saw that its title on the mainlist is just "Goat." I cannot explain why that is as funny as it is. Anyway, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pro tip: if you want people to like your skip, include a giraffe skeleton.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1 giraffe skeleton (whole)

And there's the upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love how this went from "is this really a -J" to "wait, wait, WAIT! Euthanizing it killed people?!"

You sir, ma'am, or however else you prefer to be addressed, can have my +1 and eat it too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Classic cartoon goat as a SCP.

Nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
GOATSPLOSION.

Nice use of unforeseen consequences. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I DON'T KNOW WHAT YOU'RE TALKING ABOUT YOU'RE ACTING CRAZY TAGLIA THAT WAS NEVER THERE.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You fucking used "over nine thousand".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A good reminder that it pays to be careful when dealing with SCPs, especially when neutralizing them. The Foundation tends to have a pretty good idea of what these things do while they're active - neutralizing always throws in a new element to the mix. One of the main reasons the SCP Foundation isn't the Destroy Destroy Destroy Foundation. That 'Protect' part is for both sides.

Also, I really dig the phrase "massive anatomical deformation".

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goats will eat anything.
